program_name: Environmental Sciences - Global Change and Sustainability

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree, programme-specific curricular prerequisites, Teaching Committee review of the transcript and CV, preliminary online evaluation for international qualifications, and English B2. The public curriculum page on apply.unive.it still carries 2025/2026 labelling and says the information will be confirmed for 2026/2027 [uncertain].
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Minimum three-year bachelor's degree (or higher). International applicants must first request the preliminary evaluation of their qualification on apply.unive.it.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants in Biotechnologies, Biology, Agriculture and Forestry, Environmental Sciences, or Earth Sciences generally satisfy the curricular prerequisites. Applicants from other fields must have at least 60 ECTS or equivalent, with at least 18 ECTS in Chemistry, Biology and Ecology, and Earth Sciences, plus coherent coursework in Mathematics, Physics, IT, Statistics, Engineering, Law, Economics, and Agriculture as listed on the programme admission page.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: For international applications, the minimum required CGPA varies by country and ranges from 70% to 85%. On the programme admission page, applicants with the listed degree classes or equivalent and a final grade of at least 80/110 are generally considered adequately prepared.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: The Welcome Unit verifies eligibility during the preliminary evaluation. The Teaching Committee evaluates the transcript and CV and may require an additional interview if the applicant's preparation is not considered clearly adequate. Because the public curriculum page still references 2025/2026 confirmation for 2026/2027, this programme-specific summary remains partially provisional [uncertain].

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certification from an officially recognised certifying body in Ca' Foscari's recognised-certifications list.
A certificate from Ca' Foscari CLA or from a university language school included in Ca' Foscari's recognised-certifications list.
Documentary proof that the applicant falls within an official exemption category.
exemption_rules: Completed higher-education studies or at least one year of college in a country where English is the medium of instruction.
Obtained a high-school or university qualification after a course of study taught entirely in English.
Completed at least one full year of high-school exchange in English.
Holds one of the listed language-degree exemptions with a three-year English study path.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Applicants may apply before obtaining the certificate, but they must provide recognised proof of English to complete enrolment. Ca' Foscari does not conduct an English test as part of the application procedure. The public curriculum page used for this curriculum is still labelled 2025/2026 [uncertain].

rounds: Applications for 2026/2027 open from 17 December 2025 on apply.unive.it. The portal's EU or equivalent round table is nationality-routed and the detailed table is linked externally, so the accessible official pages under the allowed domains expose only the overall master's final application deadline of 17 June 2026 [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Enrolment from 1 July 2026 to 30 September 2026; additional enrolment period from 1 October 2026 to 18 December 2026 with a EUR 50 surcharge [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu.notes: The public Environmental Sciences programme site still resolves to an Enrolment from 2025/2026 interface, even though the admission page body contains 2026 evaluation sessions and enrolment dates. The full per-round EU table is not reproduced on the allowed official pages [uncertain].

rounds: The detailed 2026/2027 study-visa admission-round table is not reproduced on the allowed official pages; the official portal instead states that applications are closed [uncertain].
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Accepted or conditionally accepted applicants who require a visa must complete Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 September 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Closed for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The apply.unive.it admission-rounds article dated 4 September 2025 states that it is no longer possible for non-EU applicants requiring a visa to apply for bachelor's or master's degrees in 2026/2027. The underlying per-round cut-off dates are not reproduced on the allowed official pages [uncertain].

required_documents.notes: European applicants may use an ID card. Applications missing a mandatory identification document are rejected.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or renewal/first-request receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records for all undergraduate or bachelor's years of study, with official translation if needed
required_documents.notes: If the applicant is still attending the final year, a provisional transcript is still mandatory.
Used by the Teaching Committee to evaluate personal preparation and curricular coherence.
A dedicated motivation letter for the Global Change and Sustainability curriculum must be added to the application. The public curriculum page still carries 2025/2026 labelling [uncertain].

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree final diploma, with official translation if needed
required_documents.notes: If a provisional document was used at application stage, the final diploma must be uploaded before the end of the enrolment period.

required_documents.document_name: Statement of Comparability by CIMEA, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondance with CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement
required_documents.notes: For master's enrolment, one of these qualification-recognition documents is required; the Diploma Supplement is accepted only if it follows the international model and is issued by an institution in the EHEA.

required_documents.document_name: Recognised English B2 certificate or proof of exemption
required_documents.notes: Required to complete enrolment in this English-taught curriculum [uncertain].

required_documents.document_name: Letter of enrolment eligibility and Universitaly pre-enrolment application
required_documents.notes: Non-EU applicants requiring a visa must first receive Ca' Foscari's eligibility letter and then complete pre-enrolment on the Universitaly portal.
Once the visa is issued, it must be uploaded in the relevant task on apply.unive.it to finalize enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard entry test; additional interview may be required by the Teaching Committee
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants whose preparation is not clearly adequate on the basis of the prior degree, grades, transcript, and CV.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Three entry-requirements-evaluation sessions are published in the programme admission page body for 2026: 7 April to 4 June, 6 July to 20 August, and 1 October to 17 November, with results on 25 June, 10 September, and 4 December 2026.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: There is no standard test score. Applicants in the listed degree classes or equivalent with at least 80/110 are generally considered adequately prepared; otherwise an additional interview may be required.

source_quotes.quote: Environmental Sciences - Global Change and Sustainability
minimum required CGPA ranges between 70% and 85%
A minimum B2-level knowledge of English is mandatory
must request a preliminary online evaluation
A motivation letter must be added to your application.
it is no longer possible for non-EU applicants requiring a visa to apply

uncertainty_notes: The programme is confirmed in Ca' Foscari's 2026/2027 International programmes list, but the official degree permalink still resolves to an Enrolment from 2025/2026 interface.
The public apply.unive.it curriculum page still uses course code CMR5/GCS/2025 and states that the admission information shown for 2025/2026 will be confirmed for 2026/2027.

The detailed EU and non-EU admission-round tables for 2026/2027 are routed by nationality on apply.unive.it and linked from the official portal to external Google Drive files, which were excluded by the source policy.
